messages.content: how can I change datagridview current row when I load the form ? I'm using vb.net

To change the current row of a DataGridView when the form loads in VB.NET, you can use the SelectionChanged event handler. Here is an example of how to achieve this:

1. Start by double-clicking on the DataGridView control in your form to generate the event handler stub for the SelectionChanged event.

2. Within the event handler, add the following code to set the current row when the form loads:

```vb
Private Sub DataGridView1_SelectionChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles DataGridView1.SelectionChanged
    ' Check if the form is just loaded
    If Me.Created Then
        ' Set the desired current row index
        Dim currentRow As Integer = 2
        ' Check if the desired current row index is within the valid range
        If currentRow >= 0 AndAlso currentRow < DataGridView1.Rows.Count Then
            ' Set the current row
            DataGridView1.CurrentCell = DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Cells(0)
        End If
    End If
End Sub
```

3. In the Form_Load event handler, call the SelectionChanged event handler to set the current row:

```vb
Private Sub Form1_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    DataGridView1_SelectionChanged(Nothing, Nothing)
End Sub
```

Make sure to replace "DataGridView1" with the name of your DataGridView control, and adjust the "currentRow" variable to the desired index of the row you want to set as the current row.

With this code, the desired current row will be automatically selected when the form is loaded.
Current cell cannot be set to an invisible cell. this error happen when I used the code

I apologize for the oversight. If the desired row is not visible in the DataGridView, you will get an error when trying to set it as the current row. To handle this situation, you can use the ScrollIntoView method to make the row visible before setting it as the current row. Here's an updated version of the code:

```vb
Private Sub Form1_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    ' Set the desired row index
    Dim currentRow As Integer = 2

    ' Check if the desired row index is within the valid range
    If currentRow >= 0 AndAlso currentRow < DataGridView1.Rows.Count Then
        ' Make the row visible
        DataGridView1.FirstDisplayedScrollingRowIndex = currentRow
        ' Select the row and set it as the current row
        DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Selected = True
        DataGridView1.CurrentCell = DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Cells(0)
    End If
End Sub
```

In this updated code, the FirstDisplayedScrollingRowIndex property is used to scroll the DataGridView so that the desired row becomes visible. Then, the row is selected and set as the current row.

Make sure to replace "DataGridView1" with the name of your DataGridView control and adjust the "currentRow" variable to the desired index of the row you want to set as the current row.

This should resolve the "Current cell cannot be set to an invisible cell" error.

when I try to use a cell in the datagridview it show me this error Dim currentRow As Integer = 2

    ’ Check if the desired row index is within the valid range
    If currentRow >= 0 AndAlso currentRow < DataGridView1.Rows.Count Then
        ’ Make the row visible
        DataGridView1.FirstDisplayedScrollingRowIndex = currentRow
        ’ Select the row and set it as the current row
        DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Selected = True
        DataGridView1.CurrentCell = DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Cells(0)
    End If

I apologize for the confusion. The error you are encountering while trying to access cells in the DataGridView might be occurring due to the IndexOutOfRangeException. Please ensure that there are enough rows in the DataGridView to access the desired row and cell.

To handle this error, you can add a check before accessing the cells. Here's an updated version of the code:

```vb
Private Sub Form1_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    ' Set the desired row index
    Dim currentRow As Integer = 2

    ' Check if the desired row index is within the valid range
    If currentRow >= 0 AndAlso currentRow < DataGridView1.Rows.Count Then
        ' Make the row visible
        DataGridView1.FirstDisplayedScrollingRowIndex = currentRow

        ' Select the row
        DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Selected = True

        ' Check if the row has any cells
        If DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Cells.Count > 0 Then
            ' Set the first cell in the row as the current cell
            DataGridView1.CurrentCell = DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Cells(0)
        End If
    End If
End Sub

```

In this updated code, I have added a check `If DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Cells.Count > 0` to ensure that the row has at least one cell before setting it as the current cell.

Make sure to replace "DataGridView1" with the name of your DataGridView control and adjust the "currentRow" variable to the desired index of the row you want to set as the current row.

This should resolve the error you are encountering while trying to access the cells in the DataGridView.
I apologize, but it seems that your response was cut off. Can you please provide the complete details or code snippet that you would like assistance with?
Object reference not set to an instance of an object. this error apper when I use a cell in the datagridview after the load of the form how can I solve that

The "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" error typically occurs when you try to access a member or property of an object that is null.

To resolve this issue, you should ensure that the DataGridView control is properly initialized and has data before accessing its cells. Here are a few steps you can take to troubleshoot and resolve the issue:

1. Ensure that you are initializing and loading data into the DataGridView control properly. You can check if the control has data by verifying the count of rows or using the DataSource property.

2. To avoid null reference errors, always perform null checks before accessing any member or property of the DataGridView or its cells. For example:

```vb
If DataGridView1.Rows.Count > 0 AndAlso DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Cells.Count > 0 Then
    ' Access and use the cell
    Dim cellValue As String = DataGridView1.Rows(currentRow).Cells(0).Value.ToString()
    ' ... rest of your code
End If
```

In the above code snippet, the null checks ensure that the DataGridView has rows and the specific row and cell being accessed exist before attempting to access their values.

3. Verify that you are accessing the correct row and cell index. If the index is out of range, it can result in a null reference error. Make sure to adjust the index values according to your specific needs.

By following these steps, you should be able to prevent the "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" error and properly access the cells in the DataGridView without issues.
is there a problem with this  If IsDBNull(DataGridView2.CurrentRow.Cells(9).Value) = True Then the error apper agaain on this line

Yes, the error is likely occurring because the cell you are trying to access is null or DBNull. To avoid the error, you can use additional checks to handle the null or DBNull values in the cell. Here's an updated code snippet:

```vb
If DataGridView2.CurrentRow.Cells(9).Value IsNot Nothing AndAlso Not Convert.IsDBNull(DataGridView2.CurrentRow.Cells(9).Value) Then
    ' Access and use the cell value
    Dim cellValue As String = DataGridView2.CurrentRow.Cells(9).Value.ToString()
    ' ... rest of your code
End If
```

In the updated code, we first check if the cell value is not null using `IsNot Nothing`. Then, we check if the cell value is not DBNull using `Not Convert.IsDBNull()`. Only if both these conditions are satisfied, the code within the block will execute.

Make sure to adjust the column index (9 in this case) to match the actual column that you are trying to access in your DataGridView.

By incorporating these additional checks, you can handle the DBNull values and prevent the error from occurring when accessing the cell in the DataGridView.
I"m sure that the value isn't null but the error is still appering and it appear only when I don't change the current row but when I change it it works

If you are confident that the value is not null and the error only occurs when you don't change the current row, it could be related to the timing of when the code is executed. The CurrentRow property may not be updated immediately after the form is loaded.

To handle this situation, you can try postponing the code execution using the DataGridView's CellEnter event, which occurs when a cell becomes the current cell. Here's an updated code snippet:

```vb
Private Sub DataGridView2_CellEnter(sender As Object, e As DataGridViewCellEventArgs) Handles DataGridView2.CellEnter
    If e.ColumnIndex = 9 AndAlso e.RowIndex >= 0 AndAlso e.RowIndex < DataGridView2.Rows.Count Then
        If Not Convert.IsDBNull(DataGridView2.Rows(e.RowIndex).Cells(9).Value) Then
            ' Access and use the cell value
            Dim cellValue As String = DataGridView2.Rows(e.RowIndex).Cells(9).Value.ToString()
            ' ... rest of your code
        End If
    End If
End Sub
```

In this code, we handle the CellEnter event and check if the entered cell is the desired column (index 9 in this case). We also verify that the row index is within a valid range. If these conditions are met, we proceed to check for DBNull and access the cell value.

Make sure to adjust the column index (9 in this case) to match the actual column you are trying to access in your DataGridView.

Using the CellEnter event ensures that the code is executed when the cell becomes the current cell, regardless of whether it is set during form load or through user interaction. This should help avoid the error you are experiencing when accessing the cell.
if I have more that one datagridview will it affect that code ?

Yes, if you have multiple DataGridView controls on your form, the code I provided earlier will need to be adapted to handle each DataGridView separately.

To handle multiple DataGridView controls, you can assign unique event handlers to each CellEnter event for the respective DataGridView controls. Here's an updated code snippet to handle multiple DataGridView controls:

```vb
Private Sub DataGridView1_CellEnter(sender As Object, e As DataGridViewCellEventArgs) Handles DataGridView1.CellEnter
    If e.ColumnIndex = 9 AndAlso e.RowIndex >= 0 AndAlso e.RowIndex < DataGridView1.Rows.Count Then
        If Not Convert.IsDBNull(DataGridView1.Rows(e.RowIndex).Cells(9).Value) Then
            ' Access and use the cell value
            Dim cellValue As String = DataGridView1.Rows(e.RowIndex).Cells(9).Value.ToString()
            ' ... rest of your code
        End If
    End If
End Sub

Private Sub DataGridView2_CellEnter(sender As Object, e As DataGridViewCellEventArgs) Handles DataGridView2.CellEnter
    If e.ColumnIndex = 9 AndAlso e.RowIndex >= 0 AndAlso e.RowIndex < DataGridView2.Rows.Count Then
        If Not Convert.IsDBNull(DataGridView2.Rows(e.RowIndex).Cells(9).Value) Then
            ' Access and use the cell value
            Dim cellValue As String = DataGridView2.Rows(e.RowIndex).Cells(9).Value.ToString()
            ' ... rest of your code
        End If
    End If
End Sub
```

In this updated code, there are two separate event handlers for CellEnter events, one for DataGridView1 and another for DataGridView2. Each event handler is responsible for handling the specified DataGridView's cell enter event, checking the desired column (index 9 in this case), and accessing the cell value.

Make sure to adjust the column index (9 in this case) and the DataGridView names (DataGridView1 and DataGridView2) to match the actual columns and names of your DataGridView controls.

By implementing separate event handlers for each DataGridView control, the code will handle each DataGridView independently, ensuring that the correct cells are accessed without interference between the DataGridView controls.
forget what I said I have new question when I select any cell it selects the whole row I don't want that I want to select a certain cell

To prevent the whole row from being selected when you click on a cell in a DataGridView, you can disable the FullRowSelect property and enable the CellSelect property. This will allow you to select individual cells instead of the entire row. Here's how you can achieve this:

1. In the properties window of the DataGridView control, set the `SelectionMode` property to `CellSelect`.

2. Set the `MultiSelect` property to `False` if you only want to select a single cell at a time. If you want to allow multiple cells to be selected, you can set `MultiSelect` to `True`.

With these settings, clicking on a cell in the DataGridView will select only that particular cell, and the whole row will not be selected.
